tags.name: `mc-time-picker`
tags.description:
Purpose: A time picker component for selecting hours and minutes.
Category: Forms
When to use: When you need to allow users to select a specific time.
When not to use: When you only need to capture a single time value without user interaction.
Works with components: mc-button, mc-icon, mc-popover
Alternative component names: Time Picker, Time Selector

Events:

  * `timeselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Emitted upon every minute/hour selection.

Attributes:

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the input.

  * `hourstep` {`number`} - A numeric value that indicates the level of granularity that the hour items must meet.
It can range between greater than 1 and less than 23. For more details, see the examples.

  * `minutestep` {`number`} - A numeric value that indicates the level of granularity that the minute items must meet.
It can range between greater than 1 and less than 59. For more details, see the examples.

  * `preselectcurrenttime` {`boolean`} - True, if the current local time must be preselected.

  * `trapfocus` {`boolean`} - False, if set to true focus will be trapped inside the component.

  * `value` {`string | null | undefined`} - ':' separated string representing the time which should be chosen.

  * `preventinitialeventdispatch` {`boolean`} - True if the timeselected event shouldn't be
dispatched on initial load when used in a popover.

Properties:

  * `firstUpdate` {`boolean`} - 

  * `hoursData` {`{ label: string; value: string; }[]`} - 

  * `minutesData` {`{ label: string; value: string; }[]`} - 

  * `hourPicker` - 

  * `minutePicker` - 

  * `hour` {`string`} - 

  * `minute` {`string`} - 

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the input.

  * `hourstep` {`number`} - A numeric value that indicates the level of granularity that the hour items must meet.
It can range between greater than 1 and less than 23. For more details, see the examples.

  * `minutestep` {`number`} - A numeric value that indicates the level of granularity that the minute items must meet.
It can range between greater than 1 and less than 59. For more details, see the examples.

  * `preselectcurrenttime` {`boolean`} - True, if the current local time must be preselected.

  * `trapfocus` {`boolean`} - False, if set to true focus will be trapped inside the component.

  * `value` {`string | null | undefined`} - ':' separated string representing the time which should be chosen.

  * `preventinitialeventdispatch` {`boolean`} - True if the timeselected event shouldn't be
dispatched on initial load when used in a popover.

  * `timeselected` - Emitted when a new time is selected

attributes.name: fit
attributes.description:
`fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the input.

Property: fit

Default: medium

attributes.name: hourstep
attributes.description:
`hourstep` {`number`} - A numeric value that indicates the level of granularity that the hour items must meet.
It can range between greater than 1 and less than 23. For more details, see the examples.

Property: hourstep

Default: 1

attributes.name: minutestep
attributes.description:
`minutestep` {`number`} - A numeric value that indicates the level of granularity that the minute items must meet.
It can range between greater than 1 and less than 59. For more details, see the examples.

Property: minutestep

Default: 1

attributes.name: preselectcurrenttime
attributes.description:
`preselectcurrenttime` {`boolean`} - True, if the current local time must be preselected.

Property: preselectcurrenttime

Default: false

attributes.name: trapfocus
attributes.description:
`trapfocus` {`boolean`} - False, if set to true focus will be trapped inside the component.

Property: trapfocus

Default: false

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
`value` {`string | null | undefined`} - ':' separated string representing the time which should be chosen.

Property: value

Default:

attributes.name: preventinitialeventdispatch
attributes.description:
`preventinitialeventdispatch` {`boolean`} - True if the timeselected event shouldn't be
dispatched on initial load when used in a popover.

Property: preventinitialeventdispatch

Default: false
